response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack and web engineer with broad experience across TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, but the profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position requires native-level Japanese, strong C for UNIX development, Linux kernel source analysis and modification, and hands-on embedded networking security (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching) — none of which appear in the resume. C/C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments and a thesis project, the listed languages are English, Romanian, and German with no Japanese, and there is no evidence of switch, firmware, or kernel-level work. The gap on hard requirements is too large to justify an interview.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career spanning web, mobile, desktop, and some hardware-adjacent work
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, teaching and workshops)
Some systems-level exposure: C/C++ at National Instruments, low-latency DLang services, Linux and audio-server work
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed, which is a hard requirement (native level)
No embedded firmware, Linux kernel, or network-switch experience; career centers on web and full-stack development
No demonstrated security-technology background (MACsec, 802.1X, authentication systems, encryption, tamper detection)

key_factors.factor: Solid general engineering track record and craftsmanship
